metadata.title: County Property Tax Rates and Veteran Exemptions — IL
metadata.description: County-level property tax rates, veteran exemption implementation details, and application information for high-veteran-population counties in Illinois.
sources: 35 ILCS 200/15-165 Disabled Veterans Standard Homestead Exemption

notes.tax_rate_format: Effective rate per $100 of assessed value (applied to market value).
notes.assessment_note: Illinois assesses residential property at 33.33% of market value (EAV). Effective rates shown are market-value-based.
notes.veteran_exemption_note: Illinois 35 ILCS 200/15-165: Exemption on up to $100,000 of equalized assessed value (EAV) for 100% disabled veterans. EAV = ~33% of market value. Effectively full exemption for homes with market value ≤~$300,000. Partial benefit for higher-value properties.
notes.update_cycle: Tax rates set annually by county and municipal authorities.

property_tax.notes: Cook County (Chicago area). IL's highest effective property tax rate. Assessment is 10% of market for residential.

disabled_veteran_100_pct.eligibility: 100% disabled veteran or IU at 100%; IL resident; primary residence
disabled_veteran_100_pct.savings_basis: $100,000 EAV exemption × 2.08% Cook County effective rate. For homes ≤~$300K market value, full exemption. For $320K median home: ~$2,080 savings.
disabled_veteran_100_pct.note: Exemption on up to $100,000 of EAV (35 ILCS 200/15-165). Cook County EAV ≈ 10% of market value (not 33%) due to local assessment rules. A $320K home has ~$32,000 EAV — fully exempted. Savings = full tax bill for most Cook County homes.

documentation: VA Summary of Benefits Letter or Rating Decision
Proof of IL residency and ownership

property_tax.notes: Lake County (northern IL, home of Naval Station Great Lakes). High property tax rate.

disabled_veteran_100_pct.eligibility: 100% disabled veteran or IU at 100%; IL resident; primary residence
disabled_veteran_100_pct.savings_basis: $100,000 EAV exemption × 2.65% effective rate. Homes ≤~$300K market value receive full exemption.
disabled_veteran_100_pct.note: Naval Station Great Lakes retirees: Lake County has one of the highest rates in IL. $100,000 EAV exemption (35 ILCS 200/15-165) covers full EAV for most local homes. Apply with Lake County Assessor.

documentation: VA Rating Decision or Summary of Benefits Letter
Proof of IL residency and ownership

property_tax.notes: St. Clair County (Metro East, IL). Home of Scott AFB — very high veteran population relative to county size.

disabled_veteran_100_pct.eligibility: 100% disabled veteran or IU at 100%; IL resident; primary residence
disabled_veteran_100_pct.savings_basis: Median home $170,000 × 2.15% = $3,655. Full exemption as EAV (~$56,700) is well below $100,000 EAV cap.
disabled_veteran_100_pct.note: Scott AFB area: Most homes are below the $300K threshold where the $100,000 EAV exemption provides full relief. Effectively a full exemption for the majority of Scott AFB veterans and retirees in this county.

application.office: St. Clair County Supervisor of Assessments
documentation: VA Rating Decision or Summary of Benefits Letter
Proof of IL residency and ownership

property_tax.notes: Sangamon County (Springfield, IL state capital area).

disabled_veteran_100_pct.eligibility: 100% disabled veteran or IU at 100%; IL resident; primary residence
disabled_veteran_100_pct.savings_basis: Median home $175,000 × 1.90% = $3,325. Full exemption as EAV (~$58,300) is well below $100,000 EAV cap.
disabled_veteran_100_pct.note: Effectively a full exemption for most Sangamon County homes (EAV well below $100,000 cap). Apply with Sangamon County Assessor.

documentation: VA Rating Decision or Summary of Benefits Letter
Proof of IL residency and ownership

va_facilities: Danville VA Medical Center (VA Illiana Health Care System)

property_tax.notes: Vermilion County (Danville). Illinois assesses at 33.33% of market value with state equalization factor. Home to the Danville VAMC, a major regional VA health care facility.

disabled_veteran_standard.eligibility: VA service-connected disability; IL resident; primary residence
disabled_veteran_standard.note: Illinois Disabled Veterans' Standard Homestead Exemption (35 ILCS 200/15-169). Veterans 70%+ effectively receive full exemption on most homes.
